denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 04/06/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100578087          Group #: GRP-1073
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-114022
Claim #: CLM-2026-191870
Date of Service (requested): 03/17/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services
 Dear Plan Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/19/2026 for CT pulmonary angiography related to Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: CT pulmonary angiography
Diagnosis (as submitted): Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service. Clinical policy applied: CPB 0133 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0133 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100578087) and reference PA-2026-114022. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99). She has been treated under commercial coverage with CT pulmonary angiography recommended as the next appropriate step. Wells score 4.5; hypoxia 91% on room air; D-dimer elevated. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for CT pulmonary angiography in the setting of Pulmonary embolism, suspected low-intermediate risk (I26.99); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
